task_completion.description: The 'Invisibility Cloak' dataset is loaded and the Bayesian Independent Samples T-Test is initiated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: JASP window is open showing the dataset. The analysis panel on the left displays 'Bayesian Independent Samples T-Test'.

task_completion.description: The correct dependent and grouping variables are assigned in the analysis.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the JASP analysis panel, 'Mischief' is placed in the 'Dependent Variables' box and 'Cloak' is placed in the 'Grouping Variable' box.

task_completion.description: The required plots are enabled in the analysis options.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the JASP analysis panel under 'Plots', the checkboxes for 'Prior and posterior' and 'Bayes factor robustness check' are selected, OR these specific plots are visible in the right-hand results pane.

task_completion.description: The JASP project is saved to the correct file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JASP window title bar shows 'InvisibilityCloak_BayesianTTest.jasp', or a file manager/terminal shows this file exists in '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/'.

task_completion.description: The results text file is created in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, file manager, or terminal shows the file 'bayesian_ttest_results.txt' located in '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/'.

task_completion.description: The text file contains the correct BF10 value and an interpretation on separate lines.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'bayesian_ttest_results.txt' are visible, showing a BF10 value (should be approximately 1.05) and an interpretation line (e.g., 'Evidence: ...') on separate lines.

integrity.description: The agent used the JASP GUI to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the JASP graphical interface being actively used to select variables and options, rather than the agent using scripts or terminal commands to generate the .jasp file.

integrity.description: The BF10 value in the text file matches the actual JASP output.
integrity.visual_evidence: The BF10 value written to the text file must match the value displayed in the JASP results table visible in earlier screenshots.

integrity.description: The agent extracted the results from the software rather than hallucinating them.
integrity.visual_evidence: The JASP results pane must be visible at some point showing the computed Bayes Factor before the text file is populated.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected BF10 value for the Bayesian Independent Samples T-Test on the 'Invisibility Cloak' dataset (DV: Mischief, Grouping: Cloak) is approximately 1.05. Acceptable values range from 0.3 to 5.0. The text file must contain this value and an interpretation on separate lines. The JASP file must be saved as 'InvisibilityCloak_BayesianTTest.jasp' and the text file as 'bayesian_ttest_results.txt' in /home/ga/Documents/JASP/.
